_comment: v0.8 Tier-2 AUTHORED game-specific guides. These override the per-emulator base guide for these games (resolver picks lowest tier number). Each one adds game-specific knowledge — required mods, the exact preset, the per-title gotchas — on top of the generic emulator setup. Written against known-good community configs as of dataAsOf. emulatorId must match the game's recommended emulator so the resolver surfaces it.

steps.text: Install Winlator Cmod (latest).
Install Turnip 24.3.0 — Skyrim's DX11 path is stable on it.
Create a 'Bethesda' container with these exact settings.

settings.Video memory: Set to ~12 GB so HD texture mods don't crash

steps.text: Copy your Skyrim SE folder (the one with SkyrimSE.exe) into the container.
Launch via SkyrimSELauncher.exe ONCE to let it detect settings, then launch SkyrimSE.exe directly afterwards for faster boots.
If you mod: install SKSE matching your exact Skyrim build number, and add '-windowed -borderless' to the launch arguments for stability on mobile.
Run SkyrimSE.exe from the container. Expect a near-locked 50-60 fps at 720p on a high-end Snapdragon.

troubleshooting.symptom: Crash to desktop on the Bethesda logo
troubleshooting.fix: SKSE version mismatch — reinstall the SKSE build that matches your Skyrim version exactly, or remove SKSE to test vanilla.
Lower shadow distance in-game and cap FPS to 60 — uncapped physics break above 60 fps in this engine.
Drop DX wrapper to DXVK 2.3 and relaunch.

steps.text: Install GameNative — its Proton support handles Elden Ring's EAC better than vanilla Winlator.
Install Turnip 24.3.0.
Container settings for Elden Ring.
No BIOS. But Elden Ring uses EasyAntiCheat — you must launch in OFFLINE/seamless-coop mode. Online play with EAC will not work and risks a ban.
Copy your Elden Ring folder in. Use the offline launcher (start_protected_game bypass or the Seamless Co-op mod's launcher).
Cap to 30 fps in the config — Elden Ring is hard-locked to 60 and runs ~25-30 on a high-end Snapdragon. A stable 30 feels far better than a fluctuating 30-45.
Launch offline. Lower settings to Low/Medium, 720p.

troubleshooting.fix: You're launching the online exe. Use the offline/seamless-coop launcher — never the EAC online path on emulation.
Confirm VKD3D is on (DX12 game) and lower texture quality; the GPU runs out of memory at high textures.
Expected on demanding scenes. Drop to 720p Low and cap 30 fps. This title is borderline even on flagship mobile.

steps.text: Install GameNative.
Install Turnip 24.3.0.
Witcher 3 settings.

settings.DXVK: On (use the DX11 'Classic' build, not Next-Gen DX12)

steps.text: Copy your install. Prefer the 1.32 'Classic' (DX11) build — the Next-Gen 4.0 (DX12) update is much heavier on mobile.
Launch witcher3.exe at 720p Medium. ~35-45 fps on the Classic build.

troubleshooting.fix: You're probably on the Next-Gen DX12 build. Roll back to the 1.32 Classic DX11 build for far better mobile performance.

steps.text: Install Winlator Cmod.
Hollow Knight is a light Unity game — it runs great.
Copy your Hollow Knight folder in.
Launch hollow_knight.exe. Locked 60 fps at 1080p is easily achievable.
This is a great first game to test your Winlator setup — if Hollow Knight doesn't run, something's wrong with your container, not the game.

troubleshooting.fix: Install the dotnet/mono component in the container settings, then relaunch.

steps.text: Install patched NetherSX2.
PS2 BIOS required. Place it in the bios folder. This comes from a PS2 you own — there's no download here; place it once you have it.
God of War runs near-perfect with these settings.

settings.Blending: Basic (High if you see transparency issues)

steps.text: Add your God of War .iso/.chd.
Launch — expect a locked 60 fps at 3x on a high-end Snapdragon.

troubleshooting.fix: Raise the blending accuracy to High in the renderer settings — God of War uses heavy blend effects.

steps.text: Install patched NetherSX2.
PS2 BIOS required. Place it in the bios folder. This comes from a PS2 you own — there's no download here; place it once you have it.
SotC is demanding for PS2; tune carefully.

settings.Half-pixel offset: Special (Texture) — fixes the signature bloom haze

steps.text: Add your SotC .iso/.chd.
Launch. 40-60 fps at 2x; the original game itself dipped on real PS2, so some slowdown is authentic.

troubleshooting.fix: Set half-pixel offset to Special (Texture) — this is the classic SotC fix.

steps.text: Install Eden.
Switch keys (prod.keys) and firmware are required. These come from a Nintendo Switch you own — there's no download for them. Import them via Eden's key/firmware installer once you have them.
Install Turnip 24.3.0 — Smash is very driver-sensitive.
Smash Ultimate settings.
Add your dumped Smash .nsp/.xci + update.
Play training mode for 2-3 minutes first to build the shader cache, then real matches run smooth. ~50-60 fps after caching on a high-end Snapdragon.
Apply firmware + keys, install the title update, launch.

troubleshooting.symptom: Stutter on first match of every character/stage
troubleshooting.fix: Normal — shaders compile on first sight. Async shaders + disk cache make subsequent matches smooth.
Confirm you installed the game UPDATE (Smash needs its latest patch) and that firmware matches your keys.

steps.text: Install Eden.
Switch prod.keys + firmware required. Import via Eden's installer.
Install Turnip 24.3.0.
Mario Odyssey settings — one of the best-running first-party titles.
Add your dumped Odyssey .nsp/.xci.
Launch — frequently a locked 60 fps, often at 2x, on a high-end Snapdragon.

troubleshooting.symptom: Audio stutter in busy areas (New Donk City)
troubleshooting.fix: Drop resolution to 1x in the densest areas; the bottleneck is GPU, not the emulator.

steps.text: Install Eden.
Switch prod.keys + firmware required. Import via Eden's installer.
Install Turnip 24.3.0.
Metroid Dread is well-optimised and runs great.
Add your dumped Dread .nsp/.xci.
Launch — solid 60 fps even at 2x-3x.
Install PPSSPP.
Chains of Olympus settings.
Add your God of War: Chains of Olympus .iso/.cso.
Launch — flawless locked 60 fps at 5x on any modern device.
Install Winlator Cmod.
Hades is a light, gorgeous 2D game — runs perfectly.
Copy your Hades folder in.
Launch Hades.exe — locked 60 fps at 1080p.
Install GameNative.
Install Turnip 24.3.0.
Cyberpunk is extremely heavy — set expectations low.
Copy your install. Launch with the DX11 flag if the game offers one — DX12 is brutal on mobile.
Realistically 15-25 fps at 720p Low on a high-end Snapdragon. This is a 'technically runs' title, not a 'plays well' one. Cap 30 and lower everything.
Launch at 720p, Low preset, cap 30 fps.

troubleshooting.symptom: Crash on load / out of memory
troubleshooting.fix: Set video memory to your full RAM headroom, drop textures to Low, and close all background apps.
Force DX11 mode and 720p Low. Even then this is one of the hardest PC titles to run on mobile.

steps.text: Install Winlator Cmod.
Stardew is trivially light. Note: a native Android version exists — emulation is only needed for the PC mod ecosystem (SMAPI).
Copy your Stardew folder in. For mods, install SMAPI inside the container.
Launch — locked 60 fps. Use this to run the modded PC version with SMAPI.
If you don't mod, just buy the native Android version — it's smoother and made for touch. Emulate only for SMAPI mods.
Install Winlator Cmod.
Install Turnip 24.3.0.
Dark Souls Remastered settings.
Play OFFLINE. DSR online uses anti-cheat that won't work on emulation.
Copy your install. Launch DarkSoulsRemastered.exe offline.
Launch offline at 720p — ~45-60 fps on a high-end Snapdragon.

troubleshooting.fix: Disable network or set the game offline — DSR's matchmaking hangs under emulation.
